title: luzula forsteri subsp. caspica
intro: Luzula forsteri subsp. caspica, also known as Caspian Woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is a perennial plant with narrow, grass-like leaves and purple-brown flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and treat urinary issues. In herbal preparations, Luzula forsteri subsp. caspica is often used as a tea or tincture to support kidney health and promote digestion, although its effectiveness and safety may vary and should be researched further.
